name: run
context: Execute an assistant on a thread — create runs, poll for completion, stream results, and handle tool calls
info: Runs execute an assistant against a thread's messages. Create a run via `POST /v1/threads/{thread_id}/runs` with the assistant ID. The run processes the thread's messages, invokes tools if needed, and appends the assistant's response. Runs are asynchronous — poll with `GET /v1/threads/{thread_id}/runs/{run_id}` until the status is terminal (`completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`, `expired`). The `requires_action` status means the assistant wants to call functions — extract tool calls, execute them, and submit outputs with `POST .../runs/{run_id}/submit_tool_outputs`. The SDK provides `createAndPoll()` for automatic polling and `stream()` for real-time Server-Sent Events. Runs support `max_prompt_tokens` and `max_completion_tokens` to control costs, `truncation_strategy` for context management, and `additional_instructions` for per-run overrides.
triggers: run an assistant on a thread
handle tool calls in a run

sections.title: Creating runs
sections.body: Call `client.beta.threads.runs.create(threadId, { assistant_id })` to start a run. Optional parameters: `additional_instructions` (appended to assistant instructions), `additional_messages` (messages to add before the run), `temperature`, `max_prompt_tokens`, `max_completion_tokens`, `truncation_strategy`, `tool_choice` (auto/none/required/specific tool), `response_format`. The run starts asynchronous processing immediately.

sections.title: Polling for completion
sections.body: Use `createAndPoll()` for automatic polling with exponential backoff. For manual polling, call `runs.retrieve(threadId, runId)` in a loop checking `run.status`. Terminal statuses: `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`, `expired`. The `in_progress` and `queued` statuses mean the run is still executing. Poll every 1-2 seconds for typical runs.

sections.title: Handling tool calls
sections.body: When `status` is `requires_action`, extract tool calls from `run.required_action.submit_tool_outputs.tool_calls`. Each tool call has `id`, `function.name`, and `function.arguments` (JSON string). Execute the functions, then submit results with `runs.submitToolOutputs(threadId, runId, { tool_outputs })` or `submitToolOutputsAndPoll()`. Outputs not submitted within 10 minutes cause the run to expire. See [[openai/function-calling]] for defining tools.

sections.title: Streaming runs
sections.body: Use `runs.stream()` for real-time Server-Sent Events. Key events: `thread.run.created`, `thread.message.delta` (incremental text), `thread.run.requires_action` (tool calls), `thread.run.completed`, `thread.run.failed`. The `thread.message.delta` event contains `data.delta.content` with incremental text parts. Streaming can also be used with `submitToolOutputsStream()` after handling tool calls.
